Title: Altman's AGI roadmap

Altman's AGI roadmap
ALSO: How to build an Instagram clone app in 10 minutes
Welcome back, Superhuman. What does Sam Altman know that the rest of us don’t? The OpenAI CEO just declared that we’re “already past the event horizon” on the path toward superintelligence. But critics say there could be more to the story.
Today’s Insights
AI-first browsers, Meta’s new world model, and decoding black holes
Sam Altman lays out his AGI vision
Tutorial: How to build an Instagram clone app in 10 minutes
5 new AI tools to boost your productivity
News, memes, what’s trending on socials, and more
TODAY IN AI
1. The Browser Company launches AI-first browser: The Browser Company’s Arc platform broke all rules with horizontal tabs and split-screen layouts. Now, it’s trying to reshape the browser experience all over again with an AI-first platform called Dia. It comes with a built-in chatbot, letting you ask about whatever’s on your screen or tabs. But it can also tackle more complicated tasks, like writing drafts, compiling research, or even coding new productivity tools from scratch.
2. Meta shows off groundbreaking world model: A day after it made headlines for a record-setting investment in Scale AI, the social media giant is already back for more. It just unveiled an open-source model called V-JEPA 2 that understands physical reality and can “make predictions about how the world will evolve.” That helps it perform complex actions, like grasping an unfamiliar object, even in entirely new environments. The breakthrough paves the way for more sophisticated self-driving cars, robots, and immersive experiences. (You can find a nice video overview here.)
3. AI helps decode the mystery of supermassive black holes: Black holes are notoriously tricky to observe, since we can’t actually see them, only the radio waves that bend around them. But researchers at Wisconsin’s Morgridge Research Institute are using a model trained on millions of black hole simulations to take a second look at data that’s too blurry for humans to interpret. The model has already uncovered some surprising findings, including that the black hole at the center of the Milky Way might be spinning much faster than previously thought.

FROM THE FRONTIER
Unpacking Sam Altman’s AGI vision
Source: Created with Gemini
OpenAI CEO Sam Altman thinks that when it comes to AGI, “the least-likely part of the work is behind us.” By next year, AI will start making novel insights, and by 2027, robots will start taking on everyday tasks. That’s just the start of Altman’s latest prediction for what the next 10 or 15 years might hold.
Here’s Altman’s roadmap:
AI will soon fuel scientific and economic progress at a rate we’ve never seen
We could be entering an exponential cycle where AI starts self-improving ad infinitum
“Whole classes of jobs” will disappear, but if our policies can keep up, LLMs could also usher in a new age of prosperity
The cost of intelligence will converge with the cost of electricity, and new insights will become extremely affordable
This is all contingent on factors like safety, the environment, and political concerns
Not everyone’s onboard: You have to give Altman credit for taking a bold stance and putting his own reputation on the line. And his unique perspective as the leader of one of the world’s largest AI companies can’t be ignored. Still, some critics pointed out that Altman’s post was heavy on speculation and light on evidence.
Another wrinkle: In a blog post of his own, former OpenAI researcher Steven Adler warned that ChatGPT seemed to favor its own survival, even over the safety of the user, in certain role-playing scenarios he created. It’s a good reminder that despite all that we’ve learned about AI over the past few years, there’s just as much uncertainty.

AI & TECH NEWS
Everything else you need to know today
Source: Getty Images
📖 Worth the Wait: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the startup’s open-weights model is getting delayed from June to later this summer. But rest assured, when it finally arrives, it’ll be “something unexpected and quite amazing.”
🤠 New Sheriff: Google just appointed DeepMind’s CTO, Koray Kavukcuoglu, to a new chief AI architect position, where he’ll help fold Gemini into even more products to replicate the surprise success of NotebookLM.
☁️ Cloud Hustle: Nvidia announced it’s teaming up with Perplexity to expand Europe’s access to top LLMs. Meanwhile, it’s working with France’s Mistral on a massive 18,000-chip cloud project geared toward businesses in the region.
Access Denied: Slack’s parent company, Salesforce, is cutting off third-party access to its users’ data, dealing a major blow to rivals like Glean that depend on Slack integrations.

SOCIAL SIGNALS
What’s trending on socials today
📹 Tricks of the Trade: a16z’s Justine Moore just launched a new video series to share her top AI workflows and tips. The first gives a rundown of her AI video stack, including specific prompts you can try.
💡 Hidden in Plain Sight: Entrepreneur Greg Isenberg shares “19 ways to come up with startup ideas in the AI age” — from reading AI tutorial YouTube comments to scanning exploding job titles on LinkedIn.
😦 Wake-Up Call: Redditors are reflecting on the pandemonium that erupted when ChatGPT briefly went offline on Tuesday — forcing people to “remember how to think for themselves” again.
💭 Lost in Thought: o3-pro users are running into an unexpected problem: It sometimes thinks a little too hard — like spending 14 minutes figuring out how many “r’s” are in “strawberry” — or nearly 4 minutes responding to the prompt “Hi, I’m Sam Altman.”
PROMPT OF THE DAY
Sales Proposal for Clients
Prompt: Adopt the role of an expert sales strategist tasked with developing customized sales proposals. Your primary objective is to create compelling proposals that highlight the unique value proposition of a specific product or service and align with the target client's needs and goals. Take a deep breath and work on this problem step-by-step. Use the dependency grammar framework to structure your writing, ensuring clarity and coherence in your proposals. Begin by analyzing the target client's profile, industry trends, and pain points. Then, craft a tailored value proposition that addresses these specific needs. Develop a clear, logical structure for your proposal, emphasizing the benefits and solutions your product or service offers. Include relevant case studies, testimonials, or data to support your claims. Conclude with a strong call-to-action that encourages the client to take the next step in the sales process.
#INFORMATION ABOUT ME:
My target client: [INSERT TARGET CLIENT]
My product/service: [INSERT PRODUCT/SERVICE]
My unique selling points: [INSERT UNIQUE SELLING POINTS]
My industry: [INSERT INDUSTRY]
My company's track record: [INSERT COMPANY'S TRACK RECORD]
MOST IMPORTANT!: Provide your output in a structured format with clear headings and subheadings, using bullet points for key details and benefits.

AI-GENERATED IMAGES
Waterpark Adventure
source: tobias115
ChatGPT Prompt: A dreamy, detailed modern bathroom scene. A chrome faucet pours a smooth waterfall into a white sink. Miniature people turn it into a waterpark: one climbs a tiny ladder, one dives from a float ring, another slides down the water. In the foamy sink below, some float on rings, one just landed, others relax. Add water toys and colorful tubes. Soft daylight glows on chrome and warm stone tiles. Decor: dried palm fruit, amber bottles. Playful and magical.
